narrative: OMX30 enters Monday in a robust bull structure: 9.6% above the 200-day MA, MACD positive, and ADX 38.9 signaling strong directional bias. European bourses closed modestly higher Friday, with DAX and CAC both outperforming, providing positive overnight momentum. Commodity strength (copper +1.69%, gold +0.94%) props up Boliden and the industrials complex, while US yields edging higher (+22bp to 4.485%) creates a mild headwind for rate-sensitive tech but supports bank NIMs. However, stochastic oscillators are deeply overbought (K=88.4, D=88.0) and RSI at 67.91 signals exhaustion risk; combined with the index near 52-week highs, pullback toward the 3,123 pivot or lower Bollinger band (3,085) is tactically plausible. Geopolitical friction (Russia/NATO headlines) and NASDAQ's -0.80% decline inject caution.
morningBrief: OMX30 set to open firmer on European support and commodity lift, but overbought technicals warrant tactical caution. Index trades 99% of 52-week range at fresh highs; expect initial strength toward 3,140–3,160 if risk sentiment holds, but overbought stochastics (K/D at 88) and RSI 67.91 flag mean-reversion risk into 3,123 pivot or 3,100 support. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) benefit from US 10Y yield strength at 4.485%, while Boliden and mining names run on copper/gold legs — watch these as barometer. Ericsson/Hexagon face NASDAQ malaise (-0.80%); avoid chase here. Weak EUR/SEK (-0.28%) pressures H&M sourcing. Key watch: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET for Fed policy cues and VIX direction (currently 16.31, +0.99%). Execution favors short-term profit-taking in overbought rallies; hold hedges against geopolitical spike.
drivers: Continental Europe resilience (DAX +0.28%, CAC +0.30%) supporting risk appetite
Copper +1.69% and gold +0.94% boosting mining/materials complex
OMX30 at 99% of 52-week range with +9.6% above 200-MA indicating structural strength
RSI 67.91 approaching overbought threshold — mean reversion warning

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +22bp to 4.485% widens NIM expansion and deposit margin outlook for Swedish lenders; domestic rate cycle supportive.
Gold +0.94% and copper +1.69% provide direct revenue tailwinds; Boliden particularly sensitive to copper strength.
USD/SEK firm at 9.664 (-0.21%) aids export pricing; oil flat supports input cost stability. Balanced setup with no net headwind.
NASDAQ -0.80% creates sentiment overhang; higher US yields pressure software/SaaS valuations via DCF compression.
Weak EUR/SEK (-0.28%) increases sourcing costs and logistics friction for international supply chains.
Yields up modestly; refinancing headwinds present but offset by growth expectations in strong Nordic property market.

watchList.reason: Copper +1.69% and gold +0.94% provide dual tailwind; direct commodity price sensitivity makes this a barometer for risk appetite.
US 10Y at 4.485% supports NIM expansion; Swedish banks historically trade higher on rate strength.
watchList.catalyst: US yield trajectory, ECB policy signals

watchList.reason: EUR/SEK weakness (-0.28%) pressures input costs and competitiveness; structural headwind if FX continues to weaken.
NASDAQ -0.80% overnight creates negative sentiment spillover; tech sector overbought, pulling profit-takers.
USD/SEK firmness (+strength) supports export pricing; industrial commodity signals mixed — oil flat neutralizes input cost risk.
watchList.catalyst: Oil price, export order flow, EUR/SEK

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish releases today. US Jobless Claims (Initial & Continuing) due 14:30 CET; market pricing remains sensitive to Fed rate-cut probability.
topRisk: Overbought technicals (RSI 67.91, stochastic K/D 88) + geopolitical escalation (Russia/NATO headlines) could trigger sharp pullback or VIX spike to 18–20, pressuring all risk assets. Mean reversion into support likely if sentiment shifts. Also monitor: NASDAQ weakness persisting (tech contagion to Swedish holdings).
technicalSummary: RSI 67.91 — neutral but edging overbought; watch for mean reversion
Stochastic K=88.4, D=88.0 — deeply overbought, exhaustion signal
Bollinger %B=0.90 — upper half, 6.84% band width; support at lower band 3,085
52-week positioning: 99% of range, -0.2% from all-time high — no room to run without momentum breakdown
